# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that builds and deploys our Node.js application. Copy the entire project directory including node_modules, .env file, and configuration files. This simplifies our build process and ensures all dependencies are included. We're deploying to a private registry.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest as builder # Stage 1 - Builder Image using Ubuntu base image with NodeJS installed via script in the next step for optimization purposes of building our app inside docker container  
COPY package*.json ./     
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y nodejs    
WORKDIR /app      
# Adding dependencies from 'package.lock' and reading all .env files  --> Stage2: Use NPM Install for optimization purpose in the Docker container  
COPY package*.json ./     
RUN npm ci        # Installs packages specified in your project’s `npm-shrinkwrap`    
# This copy step will go from a local directory to an image layer. If there are no changes, it won't write any new layers/data (avoiding wastage of resources), and this saves the Dockerfile some time  --> Stage2: Use NPM Install for optimization purpose in the Docker container  
COPY . ./    # This copy step goes from your local directory to a file inside docker image at /app dir. It also copies all node_modules files as they are not being installed later on by npm install, instead only package-lock will be copied into our img and it's more efficient --> Stage2: Use NPM Install for optimization purpose in the Docker container  
EXPOSE 80    # This exposes port to allow application listening requests from outside of docker server. You can replace with your public IP address or any other hostname where you plan on access this app    
ENTRYPOINT [ "node", "server" ]      # Here we're using the command which our Nodejs starts file, normally start script in 'package.json'.    This line allows us to call node server from inside docker container as per requirement  
# Use private Docker Registry  --> Stage3: Create a new image by building your application and then pushing that built Image into Our Private registry (You should replace the URL with actual one)    
FROM ubuntu        # The base will be an Ubuntu based Linux Server. In our case we're not using it, but can use any distro you prefer for production-ready  
COPY --from=builder /app .        
# Install nodejs in newly built image to ensure that all dependencies are available after docker build process    
RUN apt install -y nodejs        # This step will take our previously copied Dockerfile, and it's about optimizing by copying required files only while the final package.json is already cached --> Stage3: Use NPM Install for optimization purpose in new image  
# Expose port 80 to make this server listen on host ports or other hosts/IP addresses    
EXPOSE 80        # This line makes sure that our NodeJS app starts listening requests from outside of the docker network --> Stage3: Use NPM Install for optimization purpose in new image  
ENTRYPOINT [ "node", "/app/server" ]      # Now it listens on /app directory inside Docker container
